title: Can you combine the six fasts of Shawwal with making up missed Ramadan fasts?
stanceSummary: You cannot combine the six fasts of Shawwal with making up missed Ramadan fasts under a single dual intention -- they must be done separately. However, you may fast Mondays and Thursdays with the intention of making up Ramadan fasts and still receive the spirit of the sunnah reward for those days. It is better to complete missed Ramadan fasts before starting the six of Shawwal, but it is not strictly required.
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2025 video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fABBy2fBcIg) (starting at [5:51](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fABBy2fBcIg&t=351s)), Yasir Qadhi addressed whether one can combine intentions when making up missed Ramadan fasts and fasting the six days of Shawwal.

- **Mondays and Thursdays**: You may choose these days to make up Ramadan fasts and you will get the reward for following the spirit of the sunnah -- this is permissible because these are "empty slots" that can be filled with any fast
- **Six of Shawwal**: You **cannot** have dual intention -- these are a separate category, like "extra tokens" that must be purchased separately
- **Order**: It is **better** to finish making up Ramadan fasts before doing the six of Shawwal, but it is **not obligatory**
- As long as you intend to fulfill the obligatory makeup fasts, you are not sinful for doing the six of Shawwal first

---

## Full Lecture Transcript (Cleaned)

### Mondays and Thursdays with Makeup Fasts — [6:02](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fABBy2fBcIg&t=362s)

If you have fasts to make up from Ramadan, you may choose Mondays and Thursdays to follow the spirit of the sunnah and you will get the reward for doing so.

The reasoning is that Mondays and Thursdays are **"empty slots."** If you fill an empty slot with a **qada** (makeup fast), no problem -- you get both benefits.

### Six of Shawwal Are Different — [6:37](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fABBy2fBcIg&t=397s)

As for the six of Shawwal, these are **not** empty slots. These are **"extra tokens"** -- you have to buy them separately. You have to do them separately. It's not buy one, get one free.

So for the six of Shawwal, you **cannot** have dual intention. You either choose **qada** or **six of Shawwal** for any given day.

### Must You Finish Ramadan Fasts Before Shawwal? — [7:04](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fABBy2fBcIg&t=424s)

This is a common question, especially from sisters and those who were ill during Ramadan. The response: it is **better** to finish up Ramadan makeup fasts before the six of Shawwal, but it is **not necessary**.

Why? Because even if you have a **wajib** (obligation), as long as you intend to fulfill it, you are not sinful. Just as you may give **sadaqah** (voluntary charity) while still intending to pay your **zakat** next week, you may do voluntary fasts while still owing obligatory ones -- as long as you plan to fulfill the obligation.
alternateQuestions: Can I combine Shawwal fasts with making up missed Ramadan fasts?
Should I make up Ramadan fasts before fasting Shawwal?
Can I do qada fasts and Shawwal fasts at the same time?
Do makeup fasts count as Shawwal fasts?
